title: pilosella bauhini subsp. holomnoon
intro: Pilosella bauhini subsp. holomnoon is a subspecies of the Pilosella genus, commonly known as false dandelion, and is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sunflower family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Pilosella bauhini subsp. holomnoon can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be prepared by infusing the plant in hot water, extracting its active compounds with a solvent, or mixing its dried flowers with a carrier oil.
